channel.title: Posts_ENs on Lithos' Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Posts_ENs on Lithos' Blog

item.title: 🧪 Interactive Periodic Table
item.description: Interactive periodic table of elements, showing element color hue, melting and boiling point, ionization energy, electronegativity, radius, Bohr’s config, and many more.

item.title: 🐈 ArkPets suspense puzzle game (final version)
item.description: Proof of Work of a html + js puzzle-game exercise, powered by openrouter, spine-ts, opencv and d3. This project began as fabricating Arknights’ spine-cpp locally, and turned into a full-on suspense game.

item.title: 🎨 DDA3003 Visual Analytics of Global Energy Transition Patterns (Prototype)
item.description: DDA3003 Group4 Project Prototype, show global and country-wise renewable energy share on D3 globe, and cluster analysis.

item.title: 🎓 CUHKSZ course AI
item.description: Based on the CUHKSZ couse information from Kayuan and many more open-sourse platforms, combined withthe free Qwen LLM model, I present to you CUHKSZ’s own ———— LGU courseai-2.0。

item.title: 💯 CSC3060 Quiz Q&A
item.description: Since both quizes of CSC3060 is over in 2026, this blog shares the questions and answers to those quizes. You can use them as revision material for future quizes if you intend to take this course. Note that in total they take up 25% of GPA.

item.title: 🛠️ Getting Started with GitHub Action Bot for Repository Automation
item.description: Using my ‘On This Day in History’ project as an example, this post explains how a CS amateur/enthusiast university student can begin using GitHub Actions Bot to automate a remote repository, along with the common pitfalls.

item.title: 💻 CSC3060 Week 10-11 Memory Hierarchy
item.description: CSC3060 Week 10–11 at CUHKSZ: an extension of Week 1 on memory hierarchy, partitioning, and cache principles.

item.title: 🎨 DDA3003 Midterm revision
item.description: CUHKSZ elective course DDA3003 Visualization. Midterm review of InfoVis, Tufte’s design principles, Gestalt laws for visual encoding, visual perception, color schemes, D3, HTML, CSS, and multivariate data cleaning, common tables and plots.

item.title: 💻 CSC3060 Week 8-9 Pipeline and Pipeline Hazards
item.description: CSC3060 Week 8–9 at CUHKSZ: ideal pipeline implementation, advantages, and potential hazards.

item.title: 🎮 Super 'Emojio'
item.description: Proof of Work of a minimalistic html + js Super-Mario-type, rogue-like game

item.title: 💻 CSC3060 Project: Code Scheduling
item.description: CUHKSZ CSC3060 Project 3: instruction scheduling, DAG dependency graphs, and related topics.

item.title: 🕰️ Today in History
item.description:
历史上的今天 (04-15)

  • 2021 — A mass shooting occurred at a Fedex Ground facility in Indianapolis, Indiana, killing nine and injuring seven.
  • 2019 — The cathedral of Notre-Dame de Paris in France is seriously damaged by a large fire.
  • 2014 — In the worst massacre of the South Sudanese Civil War, more than 400 civilians are gunned down after seeking refuge in houses of worship as well as hospitals.
  • 2013 — Two bombs explode near the finish line at the Boston Marathon in Boston, Massachusetts, killing three people and injuring over 500 others.
  • 2013 — A wave of bombings across Iraq kills at least 75 people.

数据来源：Wikipedia On This Day。每天 UTC+1 (北京时间 9 点) 更新。

item.title: 💻 CSC3060 Week 6-7 Processor SW and HW
item.description: CUHKSZ CSC3060 Week 6: processor hardware/software architecture and how instructions are actually executed.

item.title: 💻 CSC3060 Week 5 Continuation of Machine Level Programming
item.description: CUHKSZ CSC3060 Week 5: continuing machine-level code, with calling conventions and registers.

item.title: 💻 CSC3060 Week 3-4 More about Computer System Fundamentals
item.description: CSC3060 Week 3–4 at CUHKSZ: machine-level arithmetic and machine-level code (ISA and assembly).

item.title: 🎨 DDA3003 Introduction to D3
item.description: An introduction to D3.js in CUHKSZ elective DDA3003 Visualization — an HTML-based visualization tool.

item.title: 💻 CSC3060 Week 1-2 Introduction to Computer System
item.description: CSC3060 Week 1–2 at CUHKSZ: an introduction to the hardware and software foundations of computer systems.

item.title: 💯 CSC3200 Final Revision C++ final revision handout
item.description: CSC3200 is a new CUHKSZ course, so we use a CSC3002 paper as a revision reference.

item.title: 💻 CSC3200 Final Revision: C++ Data Structures (Nonlinear)
item.description: CSC3200 at CUHKSZ: final review of trees, heaps, and graphs.

item.title: 💻 CSC3200 Final Revision: C++ Data Structures (Linear)
item.description: CSC3200 at CUHKSZ: linked lists, stacks, queues, maps, and sets.

item.title: 🧮 CSC3001 LEC17-19 function mapping
item.description: CUHKSZ CSC3001: function mappings and division rules, and how they illuminate permutations, stars-and-bars, and Catalan numbers.

item.title: 🧮 CSC3001 LEC14-16 Binomial and IE Principles, Pigeonhole Theorem
item.description: CUHKSZ CSC3001: identities of binomial coefficients, extensions of inclusion–exclusion, and the pigeonhole principle.

item.title: ⚛️ PHY1001 LEC10-15 Rotational Kinematics
item.description: Important takeaways from PHY1001 at CUHKSZ on rotational motion about an axis.

item.title: 🧮 CSC3001 LEC14-16 Graph
item.description: CUHKSZ CSC3001 图论

item.title: 🧮 CSC3001 LEC11-13 GCD and Mod
item.description: CUHKSZ CSC3001: greatest common divisors and modular arithmetic.

item.title: 🧮 CSC3001 LEC8-10 Recursion
item.description: From proof techniques to recursive thinking in CUHKSZ CSC3001, together with methods for solving recurrence relations

item.title: ⚛️ PHY1001 LEC1-5 Basic Kinematics and Newtonian Mechanics
item.description: Important takeaways from PHY1001 at CUHKSZ on basic kinematics and Newtonian mechanics.

item.title: 💻 CSC3200 LEC3-5 Intermediate C++
item.description: The second-year CUHKSZ course CSC3200 on arrays, pointers, and stream operations in C/C++.

item.title: 🧮 CSC3001 LEC6-7 Mathematical Induction
item.description: More proof techniques in CUHKSZ CSC3001

item.title: 🧮 CSC3001 LEC3-5 Set Theory, FOL, and Basic Proof Methods
item.description: An extension of propositional logic in CUHKSZ CSC3001: predicate logic

item.title: 💻 CSC3200 LEC1-2 Introduction to C++
item.description: An introduction to basic C++ syntax and OOP in the second-year CUHKSZ course CSC3200.

item.title: 🧮 CSC3001 LEC1-2 Introduction to Discrete Mathematics
item.description: An introductory guide to CSC3001, a second-year course at CUHKSZ

item.title: 🌐 How to personalize Streamlit URL?
item.description: An attempt to point a Streamlit app to a custom domain without using a public VPS.

item.title: 🌐 Automation of Ssg Post Release
item.description: How can blog publishing in an SSG setup (Hugo + Cloudflare) be semi-automated?

item.title: 🧠 Brainstorming
item.description: From a stray thought in MAT2041 to the way interdisciplinary flashes of insight can lead to breakthroughs…

item.title: 🐉🧱 CUHKSZ Survival Handbook
item.description: A survival handbook written by and for CUHKSZ students; still under active construction.

item.title: 💻 CSC1003 Final Review
item.description: A summary of useful Java syntax and common pitfalls.

item.title: 💻 CSC1003 Assignment 2: Two-Dimensional Arrays
item.description: Worked examples for the second CSC1003-Java assignment.

item.title: 💻 CSC1003 LEC13-15 Recursion
item.description: An introduction to recursion, the stack and heap, and stack overflow.

item.title: 📢 How can you batch-transcribe audio into text efficiently?
item.description: How to transcribe local audio files from the CLI without using an LLM.

item.title: 💻 CSC1003 LEC10-12 Midterm Revision
item.description: Midterm review and mock questions.

item.title: 💻 CSC1003 Assignment 1 OJ Practice
item.description: The first OJ programming practice for the first-year CUHKSZ course CSC1003.

item.title: ☕️ Coffee and OOP
item.description:
OOP (Object-Oriented Programming) is the programming paradigm built around objects. Let us use Java OOP—rather than coffee—as an example. It includes the following core ideas:

  • Class

  • Object ≈ Instance

  • Encapsulation

  • Inheritance

  • Polymorphism

  • Abstraction

Classes, instances, objects, and fields

A class is an abstract concept, whereas an instance is a concrete entity. If coffee is treated as a class, then espresso, frappe, Americano, latte, and cappuccino are instances of that class. A class is the template from which objects are created, and an instance can also be called an object. Objects are created according to the class template, but they may differ in their fields (also called member variables).

item.title: 💻 CSC1003 TBL1 Networking and the History of Computing
item.description: Foundational material on networks and the Internet that the lecture largely skipped — LGU does not really offer a dedicated introductory networking course, so many students lack this background.

item.title: 💻 CSC1003 LEC1~3 Functions, Data Types, and Computation
item.description: An introductory note to CSC1003 (Java) for first-year students at CUHKSZ.

item.title: 🌐 IP Addresses and Subnet Masks
item.description:
On 2024-08-22, several students raised a few networking questions during CUHKSZ’s course-selection training livestream (see the screenshot below). At that point the semester had not yet started, and 10.20.219.31 is obviously a private IP address, so it cannot be accessed directly from the public Internet. In other words, it can only be reached when the client is already inside the 10.0.0.0 network, namely CUHKSZ’s subnet.

Globally, the following private IP address prefixes are commonly used in local area networks (LANs). These prefixes are defined by RFC 1918:

item.title: 🌐 Attempting to Fetch CUHKSZ Mailbox Data
item.description: A shareable but unresolved attempt to automate LGU mailbox access.

item.title: 🌐 Manual for Deploying a Hugo Blog on Cloudflare
item.description: People often say that building a blog with GitHub and Cloudflare is cheap and easy. In practice, however, the process can be surprisingly full of pitfalls.
